entries.definition: The person in charge of a school.
examples: The principal announced a new rule about homework.
Our principal always greets students with a smile.

entries.definition: A group of people living in the same place or having something in common.
examples: Our town has a strong sense of community, with many local events.
The school is an important part of the local community.

entries.definition: A period of learning or teaching.
examples: We learned about fractions in our math lesson today.
The teacher prepared an interesting lesson about history.

entries.definition: A break between periods of work or study, usually for play.
examples: All the children ran outside to play during recess.
After a long lesson, everyone was ready for recess.

entries.definition: A place where books, magazines, and other materials are kept for people to read or borrow.
examples: I borrowed a new adventure book from the school library.
The town library has many computers for people to use.

entries.definition: A routine task, especially a household one.
examples: My daily chore is to feed the dog.
In old one-room schoolhouses, students often helped with chores like fetching water.

entries.definition: To have or use something with others.
examples: It's important to share your toys with your friends.
Students in one-room schoolhouses often had to share books and supplies.

entries.definition: A thought or suggestion for a possible action.
examples: She had a brilliant idea for a new game.
The principal's idea was to have school every day, all year.

entries.definition: Feeling or showing great enthusiasm and eagerness.
examples: The children were excited about the field trip.
I felt excited when I heard the good news.

entries.definition: To express dissatisfaction or annoyance about something.
examples: The students started to complain about having too much homework.
Don't complain about the weather; just put on a coat.

entries.definition: Relating to the countryside rather than a town or city.
examples: Many one-room schoolhouses were found in rural areas.
Life in a rural village can be very peaceful.

entries.definition: The process of receiving or giving systematic instruction, especially at a school.
examples: A good education is important for everyone's future.
In the past, education in rural areas often happened in one-room schoolhouses.
